sources: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes — 2024/2025
KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD Evaluation and Management
ACC/AHA Guideline for the Management of Blood Pressure — 2017 (with 2024 focused update references)

entries.text: Metformin is contraindicated at eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m^2. Dose reduction (max 1000mg/day) is recommended at eGFR 30-45. No adjustment needed above eGFR 45.
SGLT2 inhibitors are recommended in patients with T2DM and CKD with eGFR >=20 mL/min/1.73m^2 for renal and cardiovascular protection, independent of glycemic control needs.

ACEi/ARB therapy in CKD requires potassium and creatinine monitoring within 2-4 weeks of initiation or dose change; discontinue or reduce dose if serum potassium >5.5 mEq/L or eGFR falls >30% from baseline.
Blood pressure target of <130/80 mmHg is recommended for patients with diabetes or CKD to reduce cardiovascular and renal progression risk.

Moderate-to-high intensity statin therapy is recommended for patients with diabetes aged 40-75 regardless of baseline LDL, with high-intensity statin favored when additional ASCVD risk factors (e.g. CKD, hypertension) are present.

General HbA1c target is <7.0% for most non-pregnant adults; individualized higher targets (e.g. <8.0%) may be appropriate for patients with advanced CKD, limited life expectancy, or hypoglycemia risk.

NSAIDs should be avoided or used with extreme caution in patients with eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73m^2 due to risk of acute kidney injury, particularly when combined with ACEi/ARB or diuretics (triple whammy interaction).
